title: Air Quality and CO2 Monitoring (Meraki MT)
dataSources: `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:sensorreadingshistory metric="co2"` from the `cisco_meraki_sensor_readings_history` input in `Splunk_TA_cisco_meraki` (Splunkbase 5580). Key fields: `serial`, `network.name`, `metric`, `co2.concentration` (ppm), `ts`. For broader IAQ also consider `metric="tvoc"`, `metric="pm25"`, and `metric="indoorAirQuality"`.
spl:
index=meraki sourcetype="meraki:sensorreadingshistory" metric="co2"
| rename "co2.concentration" as co2_ppm
| stats latest(co2_ppm) as current_co2_ppm, avg(co2_ppm) as avg_co2_ppm by serial, "network.name"
| where current_co2_ppm > 1000
| sort - current_co2_ppm
description: Monitors indoor air quality to ensure safe working conditions.
value: Monitors indoor air quality to ensure safe working conditions.
implementation: Configure `cisco_meraki_sensor_readings_history` for MT15 (IAQ) sensors. ASHRAE recommends keeping CO₂ below 1000 ppm in occupied spaces; values above 1500 ppm correlate with reduced cognitive performance. For full IAQ, combine with TVOC, PM2.5, and the composite `indoorAirQuality.score`.
visualization: CO2 level gauge per location; trend timeline; air quality status chart.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
knownFalsePositives: IAQ excursions during high occupancy events, open windows, filter changes, or deep cleaning with solvents.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:sensorreadingshistory metric="co2"` from the `cisco_meraki_sensor_readings_history` input in `Splunk_TA_cisco_meraki` (Splunkbase 5580). Key fields: `serial`, `network.name`, `metric`, `co2.concentration` (ppm), `ts`. For broader IAQ also consider `metric="tvoc"`, `metric="pm25"`, and `metric="indoorAirQuality"`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure `cisco_meraki_sensor_readings_history` for MT15 (IAQ) sensors. ASHRAE recommends keeping CO₂ below 1000 ppm in occupied spaces; values above 1500 ppm correlate with reduced cognitive performance. For full IAQ, combine with TVOC, PM2.5, and the composite `indoorAirQuality.score`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=meraki sourcetype="meraki:sensorreadingshistory" metric="co2"
| rename "co2.concentration" as co2_ppm
| stats latest(co2_ppm) as current_co2_ppm, avg(co2_ppm) as avg_co2_ppm by serial, "network.name"
| where current_co2_ppm > 1000
| sort - current_co2_ppm
```

Understanding this SPL

**Air Quality and CO2 Monitoring (Meraki MT)** — Monitors indoor air quality to ensure safe working conditions.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:sensorreadingshistory metric="co2"` from the `cisco_meraki_sensor_readings_history` input in `Splunk_TA_cisco_meraki` (Splunkbase 5580). Key fields: `serial`, `network.name`, `metric`, `co2.concentration` (ppm), `ts`. For broader IAQ also consider `metric="tvoc"`, `metric="pm25"`, and `metric="indoorAirQuality"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: meraki; **sourcetype**: meraki:sensorreadingshistory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=meraki, sourcetype="meraki:sensorreadingshistory". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by serial, "network.name"** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where current_co2_ppm > 1000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: CO2 level gauge per location; trend timeline; air quality status chart.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how your building systems behave. We help you know if something is off with stale air, dust, and indoor comfort before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
